model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: This is essentially a coin-flip between two of the nation's most elite teams, separated by just 0.28 points in adjusted efficiency margin (Florida +33.83 vs Houston +33.55), with nearly mirror-image profiles—Florida's slightly better offense (AdjO 125.6 vs 124.6) offset by Houston's marginally superior defense (AdjD 91.0 vs 91.8). Florida's edge comes from their status as the defending national champion who beat Houston 65-63 in last year's title game, giving them a psychological advantage in a rematch of that magnitude, combined with their 1-seed positioning and the fact that their offense has the slightly higher ceiling needed to break through Houston's suffocating defense. While Houston's 28-5 record and Kelvin Sampson's tournament pedigree make the Cougars a completely credible winner, Florida's championship experience under Todd Golden and their fractionally superior offensive firepower tip this razor-thin matchup narrowly in the Gators' favor.

reasoningStep.title: South Regional Final: Florida vs Houston — A Championship Rematch
reasoningStep.summary: Two near-identical elite teams meet in a rematch of the 2025 National Championship. Florida (KenPom #4, AdjEM +33.83) and Houston (KenPom #5, AdjEM +33.55) are separated by less than 0.3 points in efficiency margin. Florida's slight offensive edge and championship pedigree from last year's 65-63 title game victory provide a razor-thin advantage.
evidence: Florida: KenPom #4, AdjO 125.6 (9th), AdjD 91.8 (5th), AdjEM +33.83, Record 26-7 SEC
Houston: KenPom #5, AdjO 124.6 (16th), AdjD 91.0 (4th), AdjEM +33.55, Record 28-5 Big 12
Florida beat Houston 65-63 in the 2025 National Championship game — direct head-to-head precedent
Houston suffered a 3-game losing streak in February with key injuries but recovered; both teams appear healthy entering the Elite Eight
Torvik confirms near-identical profiles: Florida AdjOE 126.1 (11th), AdjDE 92.2 (5th) vs Houston AdjOE 125.1 (13th), AdjDE 91.9 (4th)
Florida is the 1-seed and defending national champion; Houston is the 2-seed with the 4th-best defense nationally

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: 2026-bracket

- **Houston**: KenPom #5, AdjEM +33.55, AdjO 124.6 (16th), AdjD 91.0 (4th), Record 28-5 (Big 12)

**Historical Context**: Florida beat Houston 65-63 in the 2025 National Championship game. These teams are extremely familiar with each other.

**Analysis**:
- These teams are nearly identical in overall quality - separated by just 0.28 points in adjusted efficiency margin
- Houston has the slightly better defense (AdjD 91.0 vs 91.8)
- Florida has the slightly better offense (AdjO 125.6 vs 124.6)

- Houston had a mid-season rough patch (3-game losing streak in February with injuries to key players), but appears to have recovered
- Florida is the defending national champion and 1-seed; Houston is the 2-seed
- The prior championship game was decided by 2 points, showing how evenly matched these teams are

This is essentially a coin-flip game between two elite defensive teams. Florida's slight edge as the 1-seed, defending champion, and the team with the marginally better offense gives them a razor-thin advantage, but Houston's defensive identity and Kelvin Sampson's tournament pedigree make this incredibly close.
